A benchmark/runner exists (Go benchmark or scripted GraphQL client) that measures end-to-end `bead(id:)` latency against a fixture repo with ãË 5 projects and ãË 500 beads total. Output is a p50/p95 number, not a pass/fail bool.\n2. Baseline is recorded in the bead notes (actual number measured on the current `main` binary at the time of fix).\n3. Help about any command\r\n  init        Initialize DDx in current project\r\n  install     Install a package, resource, or the embedded DDx skills\r\n  installed   List installed packages\r\n  jq          Process JSON with jq expressions\r\n  list        List available DDx resources\r\n  log         Show DDX asset history\r\n  metric      Validate and run metric definitions\r\n  metrics     Show Manage AI prompts\r\n  search      Search available packages\r\n  server      Start the DDx HTTP and MCP server\r\n  skills      Validate bundled skill packages\r\n  status      Show version and ./cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n  \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n    \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope ã exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 ôÏ4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table ã just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet ã that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative ã ADR-018 ôÏ4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header ã `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead ã that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid ã `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure ã `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array ã `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper ã use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past ã `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future ã `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit ã `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch ã `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 ã see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` ã `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing ã `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None ã `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found ã compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection ã `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead ã the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping ã must exactly match ADR-018 ôÏ4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` ã for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ã  URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing ã all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. apply_auth_migrations_sqlite(conn: \u0026rusqlite::Connection) -\u003e Result\u003c(), String\u003e {\\n19\\t    // Create tenants table.\\n20\\t    // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\\n21\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n22\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\\n23\\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n24\\t            name               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\\n25\\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n26\\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n27\\t            updated_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL\\n28\\t user_identities table.\\n45\\t    // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\\n46\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n47\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\\n48\\t            provider       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n49\\t            external_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\\n50\\t            user_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n51\\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n52\\t            PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id)\\n53\\t        )\\\",\\n54\\t    )\\n55\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"user_identities table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n56\\t\\n57\\t    // Create tenant_users table.\\n58\\t    // PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id) is the composite PK required by ADR-018.\\n59\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n60\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_users (\\n61\\t            tenant_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\\n62\\t            user_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n63\\t            role         TEXT NOT NULL,\\n64\\t            added_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n65\\t            PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id)\\n66\\t        )\\\",\\n67\\t    )\\n68\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenant_users table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n69\\t\\n70\\t    // Create tenant_databases table.\\n71\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n72\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_databases (\\n73\\t Role-based and attribute-based access control for Axon (FEAT-012).\\n1\\t//!\\n2\\t//! # Layers\\n3\\t//!\\n4\\t//! **Layer 1 ã RBAC** ([`Role`], [`CallerIdentity`], [`Operation`])\\n5\\t//!\\n6\\t//! Four built-in roles (`Admin \u003e Write \u003e Read \u003e None`) control access to\\n7\\t//! Axon operations.  Roles are derived from the identity provider (Tailscale\\n8\\t//! ACL tags, OIDC claims, etc.) by the server layer and passed into handlers\\n9\\t//! as a [`CallerIdentity`].  Handlers call [`CallerIdentity::check`] to\\n10\\t//! enforce the minimum required role.\\n11\\t//!\\n12\\t//! **Layer 2 ã Field-level masking** ([`MaskPolicy`])\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! A `MaskPolicy` hides individual entity fields from callers whose role\\n15\\t//! falls below a configured minimum.  Applied by [`CallerIdentity::apply_masks`]\\n16\\t//! before returning entity data to the caller.  Admins always see all fields.\\n17\\t//!\\n18\\t//! **Layer 3 ã Collection write control** ([`WritePolicy`])\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! A `WritePolicy` sets a per-collection minimum write role and marks fields\\n21\\t//! as immutable after creation.  Checked by the handler before any mutation.\\n22\\t//!\\n23\\t//! **Layer 4 ã Database-scoped grants** ([`DatabaseGrant`], [`GrantRegistry`])\\n24\\t//!\\n25\\t//! Grants scope a role to a specific database (or `\\\"*\\\"` for all databases).\\n26\\t//! Without a matching grant a caller has `Role::None` on that database.\\n27\\t//! Admins with a global grant bypass per-database checks.  The grant registry\\n28\\t//! is stored in `__axon_policies__` and enforced at the routing layer.\\n29\\t\\n30\\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\\n31\\t\\n32\\tuse crate::error::AxonError;\\n33\\t\\n34\\t/// Built-in roles for Axon RBAC (FEAT-012).\\n35\\t///\\n36\\t/// Roles are ordered by privilege: `Admin \u003e Write \u003e Read \u003e None`.\\n37\\t/// When a user has multiple roles (e.g., from multiple ACL tags),\\n38\\t/// the highest-privilege role wins.\\n39\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n40\\tpub enum Role {\\n41\\t    /// No access. Explicitly denied.\\n42\\t    None = 0,\\n43\\t    /// Read entities, query, traverse, browse audit log.\\n44\\t    Read = 1,\\n45\\t    /// Create, update, delete entities and links in any collection.\\n46\\t    Write = 2,\\n47\\t    /// All operations including drop and schema changes.\\n48\\t    Admin = 3,\\n49\\t}\\n50\\t\\n51\\timpl Role {\\n52\\t    /// Parse a role from a Tailscale ACL tag or string name.\\n53\\t    ///\\n54\\t    /// Accepted inputs (both full `tag:` prefix and bare name):\\n55\\t    ///\\n56\\t    /// | Input | Role |\\n57\\t    /// |-------|------|\\n58\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-admin\\\"` / `\\\"tag:admin\\\"` / `\\\"admin\\\"` | `Admin` |\\n59\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-write\\\"` / `\\\"tag:axon-agent\\\"` / `\\\"tag:write\\\"` / `\\\"write\\\"` | `Write` |\\n60\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-read\\\"` / `\\\"tag:read\\\"` / `\\\"read\\\"` | `Read` |\\n61\\t    /// | anything else | `None` |\\n62\\t    ///\\n63\\t    /// `tag:axon-agent` is an alias for `write` ã the conventional tag for\\n64\\t    /// automated agent workloads that need read/write but not admin access.\\n65\\t    pub fn from_tag(tag: \u0026str) -\u003e Self {\\n66\\t        match tag {\\n67\\t            \\\"tag:axon-admin\\\" | \\\"tag:admin\\\" | \\\"admin\\\" =\u003e Role::Admin,\\n68\\t            \\\"tag:axon-write\\\" | \\\"tag:axon-agent\\\" | \\\"tag:write\\\" | \\\"write\\\" =\u003e Role::Write,\\n69\\t            \\\"tag:axon-read\\\" | \\\"tag:read\\\" | \\\"read\\\" =\u003e Role::Read,\\n70\\t            _ =\u003e Role::None,\\n71\\t        }\\n72\\t    }\\n73\\t\\n74\\t    /// Determine the highest-privilege role from a set of tags.\\n75\\t    pub fn from_tags(tags: \u0026[String]) -\u003e Self {\\n76\\t        tags.iter()\\n77\\t            .map(|t| Role::from_tag(t))\\n78\\t            .max()\\n79\\t            .unwrap_or(Role::None)\\n80\\t    }\\n81\\t}\\n82\\t\\n83\\timpl std::fmt::Display for Role {\\n84\\t    fn fmt(\u0026self, f: \u0026mut std::fmt::Formatter\u003c'_\u003e) -\u003e std::fmt::Result {\\n85\\t        match self {\\n86\\t            Role::None =\u003e write!(f, \\\"none\\\"),\\n87\\t            Role::Read =\u003e write!(f, \\\"read\\\"),\\n88\\t            Role::Write =\u003e write!(f, \\\"write\\\"),\\n89\\t            Role::Admin =\u003e write!(f, \\\"admin\\\"),\\n90\\t        }\\n91\\t    }\\n92\\t}\\n93\\t\\n94\\t/// The kind of operation being attempted.\\n95\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\\n96\\tpub enum Operation {\\n97\\t    /// Read an entity, query, traverse, audit.\\n98\\t    Read,\\n99\\t    /// Create, update, delete entities/links.\\n100\\t    Write,\\n101\\t    /// Drop collections, modify schemas, manage namespaces.\\n102\\t    Admin,\\n103\\t}\\n104\\t\\n105\\timpl Operation {\\n106\\t    /// The minimum role required for this operation.\\n107\\t    pub fn required_role(self) -\u003e Role {\\n108\\t        match self {\\n109\\t            Operation::Read =\u003e Role::Read,\\n110\\t            Operation::Write =\u003e Role::Write,\\n111\\t            Operation::Admin =\u003e Role::Admin,\\n112\\t        }\\n113\\t    }\\n114\\t}\\n115\\t\\n116\\t/// A simple field-equality filter used as an agent scope constraint\\n117\\t/// (FEAT-022 / ADR-016).\\n118\\t///\\n119\\t/// When attached to a [`CallerIdentity`], the agent guardrails layer rejects\\n120\\t/// any mutation whose target entity data does not satisfy\\n121\\t/// `data[field] == value`. V1 supports only field-equality predicates;\\n122\\t/// compound and range filters are deferred.\\n123\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n124\\tpub struct EntityFilter {\\n125\\t    /// The top-level data field to compare.\\n126\\t    pub field: String,\\n127\\t    /// The required value for that field.\\n128\\t    pub value: serde_json::Value,\\n129\\t}\\n130\\t\\n131\\timpl EntityFilter {\\n132\\t    /// Convenience constructor.\\n133\\t    pub fn new(field: impl Into\u003cString\u003e, value: serde_json::Value) -\u003e Self {\\n134\\t        Self {\\n135\\t            field: field.into(),\\n136\\t            value,\\n137\\t        }\\n138\\t    }\\n139\\t\\n140\\t    /// Returns `true` when the JSON object `data` satisfies this filter.\\n141\\t    ///\\n142\\t    /// Returns `false` when `data` is not a JSON object, when the field is\\n143\\t    /// absent, or when the value does not equal [`Self::value`].\\n144\\t    pub fn matches(\u0026self, data: \u0026serde_json::Value) -\u003e bool {\\n145\\t        data.get(\u0026self.field).is_some_and(|v| v == \u0026self.value)\\n146\\t    }\\n147\\t}\\n148\\t\\n149\\t/// Identity context for a request.\\n150\\t///\\n151\\t/// Populated by the auth middleware from the identity provider.\\n152\\t/// In `--no-auth` mode, all requests get `CallerIdentity::anonymous()`.\\n153\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n154\\tpub struct CallerIdentity {\\n155\\t    /// Actor name for audit log entries.\\n156\\t    pub actor: String,\\n157\\t    /// The caller's effective role (highest privilege from all their tags).\\n158\\t    pub role: Role,\\n159\\t    /// Optional scope constraint enforced by the agent guardrails layer\\n160\\t    /// (FEAT-022, ADR-016). When `Some`, this caller may only mutate entities\\n161\\t    /// whose data matches the filter. `None` means no scope restriction.\\n162\\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \\\"Option::is_none\\\")]\\n163\\t    pub entity_filter: Option\u003cEntityFilter\u003e,\\n164\\t}\\n165\\t\\n166\\timpl CallerIdentity {\\n167\\t    /// Create an anonymous identity with admin privileges (`--no-auth` mode).\\n168\\t    pub fn anonymous() -\u003e Self {\\n169\\t        Self {\\n170\\t            actor: \\\"anonymous\\\".into(),\\n171\\t            role: Role::Admin,\\n172\\t            entity_filter: None,\\n173\\t        }\\n174\\t    }\\n175\\t\\n176\\t    /// Create an identity from a name and role.\\n177\\t    pub fn new(actor: impl Into\u003cString\u003e, role: Role) -\u003e Self {\\n178\\t        Self {\\n179\\t            actor: actor.into(),\\n180\\t            role,\\n181\\t            entity_filter: None,\\n182\\t        }\\n183\\t    }\\n184\\t\\n185\\t    /// Attach an entity scope filter (builder style, FEAT-022).\\n186\\t    pub fn with_entity_filter(mut self, filter: EntityFilter) -\u003e Self {\\n187\\t        self.entity_filter = Some(filter);\\n188\\t        self\\n189\\t    }\\n190\\t\\n191\\t    /// Check whether this caller is authorized for the given operation.\\n192\\t    ///\\n193\\t    /// Returns `Ok(())` if authorized, or `Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation)`\\n194\\t    /// with a descriptive message if not.\\n195\\t    pub fn check(\u0026self, op: Operation) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n196\\t        let required = op.required_role();\\n197\\t        if self.role \u003e= required {\\n198\\t            Ok(())\\n199\\t        } else {\\n200\\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\\n201\\t                \\\"permission denied: role '{}' cannot perform {:?} operations (requires '{}')\\\",\\n202\\t                self.role, op, required\\n203\\t            )))\\n204\\t        }\\n205\\t    }\\n206\\t\\n207\\t    /// Apply field-level masking to entity data based on mask policies.\\n208\\t    ///\\n209\\t    /// Admin users see all fields. For other roles, fields listed in a\\n210\\t    /// mask policy that requires a higher role than the caller's are\\n211\\t    /// removed from the data.\\n212\\t    pub fn apply_masks(\u0026self, data: \u0026mut serde_json::Value, policies: \u0026[MaskPolicy]) {\\n213\\t        if self.role \u003e= Role::Admin {\\n214\\t            return; // Admin sees all.\\n215\\t        }\\n216\\t        if let Some(obj) = data.as_object_mut() {\\n217\\t            for policy in policies {\\n218\\t                if self.role \u003c policy.min_role {\\n219\\t                    obj.remove(\u0026policy.field);\\n220\\t                }\\n221\\t            }\\n222\\t        }\\n223\\t    }\\n224\\t}\\n225\\t\\n226\\t/// A field-level mask policy (US-046, FEAT-012).\\n227\\t///\\n228\\t/// Specifies that a field should be hidden from callers whose role is\\n229\\t/// below `min_role`. Admin always sees all fields.\\n230\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n231\\tpub struct MaskPolicy {\\n232\\t    /// The field name to mask.\\n233\\t    pub field: String,\\n234\\t    /// Minimum role required to see this field.\\n235\\t    pub min_role: Role,\\n236\\t}\\n237\\t\\n238\\t// ãã Attribute-based write control (US-047, FEAT-012) ãããããããããããããããããããããããã\\n239\\t\\n240\\t/// Per-collection write policy for attribute-based access control (ABAC).\\n241\\t///\\n242\\t/// Stored in the `__axon_policies__` pseudo-collection. Defines:\\n243\\t/// - Minimum role required to write to this collection\\n244\\t/// - Fields that are immutable after creation\\n245\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n246\\tpub struct WritePolicy {\\n247\\t    /// Collection this policy applies to.\\n248\\t    pub collection: String,\\n249\\t    /// Minimum role required for write operations.\\n250\\t    pub min_write_role: Role,\\n251\\t    /// Fields that cannot be modified after the initial create.\\n252\\t    #[serde(default)]\\n253\\t    pub immutable_fields: Vec\u003cString\u003e,\\n254\\t}\\n255\\t\\n256\\timpl WritePolicy {\\n257\\t    /// Check if a caller has sufficient role to write to this collection.\\n258\\t    pub fn check_write(\u0026self, caller: \u0026CallerIdentity) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n259\\t        if caller.role \u003e= self.min_write_role {\\n260\\t            Ok(())\\n261\\t        } else {\\n262\\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\\n263\\t                \\\"write policy for '{}': role '{}' insufficient (requires '{}')\\\",\\n264\\t                self.collection, caller.role, self.min_write_role\\n265\\t            )))\\n266\\t        }\\n267\\t    }\\n268\\t\\n269\\t    /// Check if any immutable fields have been modified.\\n270\\t    ///\\n271\\t    /// Returns the list of field names that were illegally modified.\\n272\\t    pub fn check_immutable_fields(\\n273\\t        \u0026self,\\n274\\t        old_data: \u0026serde_json::Value,\\n275\\t        new_data: \u0026serde_json::Value,\\n276\\t    ) -\u003e Vec\u003cString\u003e {\\n277\\t        let mut violations = Vec::new();\\n278\\t        for field in \u0026self.immutable_fields {\\n279\\t            let old_val = old_data.get(field);\\n280\\t            let new_val = new_data.get(field);\\n281\\t            // If the field existed before and is now different or missing, it's a violation.\\n282\\t            if old_val.is_some() \u0026\u0026 old_val != new_val {\\n283\\t                violations.push(field.clone());\\n284\\t            }\\n285\\t        }\\n286\\t        violations\\n287\\t    }\\n288\\t}\\n289\\t\\n290\\t// ãã Database-scoped access control (US-038, FEAT-014) ãããããããããããããããããããããããã\\n291\\t\\n292\\t/// A grant that scopes a role to a specific database.\\n293\\t///\\n294\\t/// Without a grant, users cannot access a database (unless they have a\\n295\\t/// wildcard `*` grant or admin role).\\n296\\t#[derive(Debug, Because `StorageAdapter` is synchronous, each database call\\n/// blocks the calling thread via `handle.block_on(...)`.\\n///\\n/// Transactions are handled via `BEGIN` / `COMMIT` / `ROLLBACK` statements.\\n/// The adapter creates the required tables on initialization if they do not\\n/// exist.\\npub struct PostgresStorageAdapter {\\n    client: tokio_postgres::Client,\\n    handle: tokio::runtime::Handle,\\n    _rt: tokio::runtime::Runtime,\\n    in_tx: bool,\\n}\\n\\nimpl PostgresStorageAdapter {\\n    /// Connect to a PostgreSQL database using a connection string.\\n    ///\\n    /// Example: `\\\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon\\\"`\\n    pub fn connect(params: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cSelf, AxonError\u003e {\\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread()\\n            .worker_threads(2)\\n            .enable_all()\\n            .build()\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let handle = rt.handle().clone();\\n        let (client, connection) = handle\\n            .block_on(tokio_postgres::connect(params, NoTls))\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(format!(\\\"connection failed: {e}\\\")))?;\\n        handle.spawn(async move {\\n            if let Err(e) = connection.await {\\n                tracing::error!(\\\"postgres connection error: {e}\\\");\\n            }\\n        });\\n        let mut adapter = Self {\\n            client,\\n            handle,\\n            _rt: rt,\\n            in_tx: false,\\n        };\\n        adapter.init_schema()?;\\n        Ok(adapter)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Block the current thread on a `tokio_postgres` future, converting any\\n    /// error into `AxonError::Storage`.\\n    ///\\n    /// When called from within a Tokio async task, uses\\n    /// [`tokio::task::block_in_place`] to temporarily un-enter the async\\n    /// context before blocking on the adapter's dedicated runtime.  This\\n    /// prevents the \\\"cannot call `block_on` from within an async context\\\"\\n    /// panic that would otherwise occur when gateway route handlers drive\\n    /// storage calls.\\n    ///\\n    /// Requires the multi-thread Tokio scheduler (used by the production\\n    /// server).  Tests that use the default `current_thread` scheduler must\\n    /// call adapter methods from a `spawn_blocking` closure.\\n    fn block\u003cT\u003e(\\n        \u0026self,\\n        fut: impl Future\u003cOutput = Result\u003cT, tokio_postgres::Error\u003e\u003e,\\n    ) -\u003e Result\u003cT, AxonError\u003e {\\n        match tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current() {\\n            Ok(_) =\u003e tokio::task::block_in_place(|| self.handle.block_on(fut)),\\n            Err(_) =\u003e self.handle.block_on(fut),\\n        }\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))\\n    }\\n\\n    fn init_schema(\u0026mut self) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n        self.block(self.client.batch_execute(\\n            \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS databases (\\n DELETE CASCADE,\\n                    name          TEXT NOT NULL,\\n                    PRIMARY KEY (database_name, name)\\n                );\\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS entities schema_name, collection, id)\\n                );\\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schemas (\\n                    collection    TEXT NOT NULL,\\n                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL,\\n                    created_at_ns BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\\n                    PRIMARY KEY (database_name, schema_name, collection, version)\\n                );\\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS collections (\\n                    name TEXT NOT NULL,\\n                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\\n                    schema_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT NULL,\\n                    view_json     JSONB NOT NULL,\\n                    updated_at_ns BIGINT NOT NULL,\\n                    updated_by    TEXT,\\n                    PRIMARY KEY NULL\\n                );\\\",\\n        ))?;\\n        self.ensure_namespace_catalog_tables()?;\\n        self.ensure_default_namespace()\\n    }\\n\\n    fn collection_exists_in_namespace(\\n We apply a conservative subset of that\\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    if name.is_empty() {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\\n            \\\"database name must not be empty\\\".to_owned(),\\n        ));\\n    }\\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\\\"non-empty checked above\\\");\\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() \u0026\u0026 first != '_' {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    Ok(())\\n}\\n\\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\\n/// `dbname` key.\\n///\\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// Both libpq keywordãvalue format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, tenant_db_name: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    let target = format!(\\\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\\\");\\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgres://\\\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgresql://\\\") {\\n        // URL format: replace path component.\\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\\\"://\\\") {\\n            let after_scheme = \u0026superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\\n                let scheme_authority = \u0026superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\\n                let rest = \u0026after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\\n                    \u0026rest[q..]\\n                } else {\\n                    \\\"\\\"\\n                };\\n                return format!(\\\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\\\");\\n            }\\n            // No path: append the target database.\\n            return format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\");\\n        }\\n        format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\")\\n    } else {\\n        // Keywordãvalue format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\\n        let mut parts: Vec\u003cString\u003e = superadmin_dsn\\n            .split_whitespace()\\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\\\"dbname=\\\"))\\n            .map(str::to_owned)\\n            .collect();\\n        parts.push(format!(\\\"dbname={target}\\\"));\\n        parts.join(\\\" \\\")\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\\n/// superadmin connection.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"CREATE DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges to execute\\n/// `DROP DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::NotFound` if the database does not exist.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\\n// Explicit verification path:\\n// }\\n\\n    impl DerefMut for TestStore {\\n        fn deref_mut(\u0026mut self) -\u003e \u0026mut Self::Target {\\n            \u0026mut self.adapter\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    fn (AuthError::CredentialMalformed, 401, \\\"credential_malformed\\\"),\\n        (AuthError::CredentialInvalid, 401, \\\"credential_invalid\\\"),\\n        (AuthError::CredentialExpired, 401, directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\\n\\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\\n```\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n```bash\\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions. data.text: The spec already has a contrast criterion, but I still need to prove the doc audit is clean for FEAT-008 rather than assume it. Iãm checking the exact audit command and whether the spec needs a more explicit edge-only contrast clause.

data.output:
---
ddx:
  id: FEAT-008
  depends_on:
    - helix.prd
    - FEAT-002
    - FEAT-014
---
# Feature: DDx Server Web UI

**ID:** FEAT-008
**Status:** In Progress
**Priority:** P1
**Owner:** DDx Team

## Overview

The DDx server (`ddx-server`) serves a web UI for browsing artifacts, beads,
the artifact dependency graph, and DDx run activity. Artifacts include markdown
documents plus sidecar-identified media such as diagrams, wireframes, images,
and PDFs. The UI is a SvelteKit application built with Svelte 5, embedded into
the Go binary via `embed.FS`, and served alongside the MCP and HTTP API
endpoints from a single process.

`ddx-server` runs as a per-user host daemon (one instance per machine, see
FEAT-002 and FEAT-020), so the same UI enumerates every project that the
host+user registry knows about and binds its navigation to one selected
project context at a time. The node-aware URL structure and combined
cross-project dashboards are specified in FEAT-021; FEAT-008 owns the
underlying views that those routes render.

The UI consumes GraphQL operations defined in `cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql`.
Every page lists its required query or subscription by name for traceability.

## Problem Statement

**Current situation:** DDx artifacts, beads, and the artifact graph are only accessible via CLI commands or MCP tool calls. There's no visual way to browse the artifact library, see the dependency graph, check bead status, regenerate generated artifacts, or review layer-aware run evidence.

**Pain points:**
- CLI output for `ddx doc graph` is text-only ã dependency graphs need visual rendering
- Bead status and dependency trees are hard to grok in terminal output
- DDx run activity has no browsable interface that preserves the `work` -> `try` -> `run` layers
- Non-markdown artifacts have no type-aware browser preview, even when the graph knows their identity
- Generated artifacts have no UI affordance for checking provenance or requesting regeneration
- New team members can't quickly understand what artifacts exist or how they relate

**Desired outcome:** A web dashboard served from the same `ddx-server` binary that already handles MCP and HTTP API. Developers open a browser, see their artifact library, dependency graph, bead board, generated-artifact provenance, and DDx run activity ã all from one URL.

## Architecture

### Single Binary, Multiple Surfaces

```
ddx-server binary
ããã /            ã Web UI (embedded SvelteKit)
ããã /api/        ã HTTP REST API (JSON) ã frozen
ããã /graphql     ã GraphQL endpoint (gqlgen)
```

The SvelteKit frontend queries the `/graphql` endpoint for all data. REST endpoints
remain unchanged and are consumed by the CLI and MCP tools only.

### Project Context

The UI is rendered under the node-scoped URL structure defined in FEAT-021:
`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/...` for project-scoped views and
`/nodes/:nodeId/...` for combined node-wide dashboards. The root `/`
redirects to `/nodes/:nodeId` using the ID returned by `GET /api/node`
(FEAT-020). When more than one project is registered the navigation bar
exposes a project picker populated from `GET /api/projects`; selecting a
project swaps the `:projectId` segment in place. All GraphQL queries issued by the
UI are bound to the selected project context.

### Project Navigation Tabs

Each `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/...` page exposes the following
project-scoped tabs in the global navigation, in this order:

| Tab | Route segment | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Overview | (none) | Project home, recent activity, operator-prompt input (ADR-021) |
| Beads | `beads` | Bead list / kanban / ready / detail (US-082*) |
| Artifacts | `artifacts` | Artifact browser (US-080, US-081b, US-083) |
| Graph | `graph` | Document dependency graph (US-081, US-081c) |
| Runs | `runs` | Layer-aware run history with `work` / `try` / `run` chips, row expansion, and re-queue (US-086b, US-086c) |
| Workers | `workers` | Live + recent worker processes for this project (US-085b, US-086, US-086a) |
| Personas | `personas` | Persona browser + role bindings (US-097) |
| Plugins | `plugins` | Plugin registry + install/uninstall (US-098) |
| Commits | `commits` | Project git commit log (US-093, FEAT-021) |
| Efficacy | `efficacy` | Model efficacy + comparisons (US-096) |

**Sessions and Executions are retired as top-level tabs** (Story 8). The
unified Runs tab carries layer chips and inline row expansion that
surface what those tabs used to show:

- `layer=run` rows expand to AgentSession transcript / billing / cached
  tokens / stderr (the old Sessions detail).
- `layer=try` rows expand to the `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` bundle
  metadata, check results, and verdict (the old Executions detail).
- `layer=work` rows expand to queue inputs and child run links.

**URL deprecation policy.** During the deprecation window, the legacy
URLs respond with **302 redirects (NOT 301)** to the matching filtered
Runs URL with all query params preserved, plus a `Sunset` response
header that names the cutoff date for the redirects:

| Legacy URL | Redirect target |
|---|---|
| `/sessions` | `/runs?layer=run` |
| `/sessions#<id>` | `/runs?layer=run&session=<id>` |
| `/executions[?bead=&verdict=&harness=&q=&after=]` | `/runs?layer=try` with the original query string preserved verbatim |
| `/executions/[id]` | `/runs/[runId]?bundle=true` |

302 (not 301) is intentional: bookmarks and external link aggregators
should not cache the redirect permanently while we still own the legacy
routes. The redirect response carries `Sunset: <RFC 9745 date>` and the
project nav drops the Sessions/Executions entries once the redirects and
the internal-link sweep land (`shellRoutes.ts`, `BeadDetail.svelte`,
commits page, agent page, Workers detail).

Manage the document dependency graph.

DDx tracks dependencies between documents using YAML frontmatter.
When an upstream document changes, DDx detects which downstream
documents are stale and need review.

Examples:
  ddx doc graph              # Show document dependency graph
  ddx doc stale              # List stale documents
  ddx doc stamp docs/prd.md  # Mark a document as reviewed
  ddx doc show helix.prd     # Show document metadata
  ddx doc deps helix.arch    # Show what a document depends on
  ddx doc dependents helix.prd  # Show what depends on a document

Usage:
  ddx doc [flags]
  ddx doc [command]

Aliases:
  doc, docs

Available Commands:
  audit       Audit document graph integrity (duplicates, missing deps, broken id_to_path)
  changed     List artifacts changed since a git ref
  dependents  Show what depends on a document
  deps        Show what a document depends on
  diff        Show content diff for an artifact
  graph       Show document dependency graph
  history     Show commit log for an artifact
  migrate     Convert legacy `dun:` frontmatter to `ddx:`
  show        Show document metadata and status
  stale       List stale documents
  stamp       Update review stamps on documents
  validate    Validate document graph (check for cycles, missing deps)

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for doc

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Use "ddx doc [command] --help" for more information about a command.

Audit document graph integrity (duplicates, missing deps, broken id_to_path)

Usage:
  ddx doc audit [flags]

Aliases:
  audit, integrity

Flags:
      --exit-zero   Always exit 0 after printing audit output
  -h, --help        help for audit
      --json        Output issues as JSON

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

data.output:
- **Performance:** Initial page load <2s. API calls <200ms. Graph renders smoothly for 100+ nodes.
- **Deployment:** Single binary. No external runtime dependencies. No separate frontend server in production.
- **Development:** `bun run dev` proxies to Go server. Hot reload for frontend. `go run` for backend.
- **Responsive:** Usable on tablet/laptop screens. Not optimized for mobile.

## User Stories

### US-080: Developer Browses Document Library
**As a** developer onboarding to a project
**I want** to open a web UI and browse all available documents
**So that** I can quickly understand what documents exist and how they're organized

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Given ddx-server is running, when I open `http://localhost:PORT`, then I see a dashboard with document counts by category
- Given I click on "Personas", then I see a list of all persona documents with names and descriptions
- Given I click on a persona, then I see its full content rendered as markdown

### US-081: Developer Views Dependency Graph
**As a** developer checking document health
**I want** to see a visual graph of document dependencies
**So that** I can identify which documents are stale and what depends on what

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Given documents have `ddx:` frontmatter with dependencies, when I open the graph view, then I see nodes and edges representing the dependency relationships
- Given some documents are stale, then stale nodes are visually highlighted (red/orange)
- Given I click a node, then I navigate to that document's detail view

### US-082: Developer Monitors Bead Status on Kanban Board
**As a** developer tracking work items
**I want** to see beads as cards on a kanban board grouped by status
**So that** I can quickly understand what's ready, blocked, and in progress

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Given beads exist, when I open the board view, then I see columns for
  open, in_progress, and closed with cards sorted by priority
- Given beads have dependencies, then blocking/blocked relationships are
  visually indicated (dimmed cards, connector lines, or grouping)
- Given I drag a card to a new column, then the bead status updates via API
- Given I click a card, then a detail panel opens showing description,
  acceptance criteria, execution runs, and the dependency graph

**E2E Test:** `artifacts.spec.ts` ã full workflow: land on dashboard ã navigate to personas ã open persona ã verify rendered content ã navigate back

### US-081: Developer Views and Navigates Dependency Graph
**As a** developer checking document health
**I want** to see a visual graph of document dependencies I can interact with
**So that** I can identify stale documents, understand relationships, and navigate directly to any doc from the graph

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Given I open the graph view, then I see nodes and edges representing dependency relationships; the graph renders with correct dimensions on first paint (no collapsed/zero-size SVG)
- Given documents are stale, then stale nodes are colored amber; documents with missing dependencies are colored red; fresh documents are green
- Given I click a node, then I navigate to that document's artifact detail view without a full page reload
- Given I press Back after clicking a node, then I return to the graph with the same zoom level and pan position restored
- Given I pan or zoom the graph, then the viewport state persists across the Back/Forward navigation cycle
- Given I apply a staleness filter chip (fresh / stale / missing), then only matching nodes and their connecting edges remain visible
- Given I apply a type filter chip, then only nodes of that media type remain visible
- Given I am on the graph page, then a `View documents` link is visible that navigates to the artifact browser filtered to markdown
- Given I am on an artifact detail page, then a `View in Graph` link navigates to the graph with that node highlighted
- Given the graph is rendered in either light or dark theme, then doc graph edges and arrowheads meet WCAG AA non-text contrast (>=3:1 against the canvas background) in both themes

**E2E Test:** `graph.spec.ts` ã full workflow: open graph ã verify non-zero render ã pan and zoom ã click stale node ã verify amber color ã click node ã verify artifact detail ã Back ã verify same viewport ã apply staleness filter ã verify node count changes ã follow `View documents` link

### US-081b: Developer Views and Regenerates Multi-Media Artifacts
**As a** developer working with generated project artifacts
**I want** the artifact browser to list all artifacts by type and render each
  one by media type, exposing regeneration when provenance exists

What changed:
- Added the explicit WCAG AA non-text contrast AC to FEAT-008 in [docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e140727a-20260505T082406-4e46ebf0/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md#L458)
- Synced the DDx-indexed canonical copy in [.agents/skills/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e140727a-20260505T082406-4e46ebf0/.agents/skills/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md#L323)
- Recorded the blocker in [.ddx/executions/20260505T082406-4e46ebf0/no_changes_rationale.txt](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e140727a-20260505T082406-4e46ebf0/.ddx/executions/20260505T082406-4e46ebf0/no_changes_rationale.txt#L1)

Why itãs blocked:
- `ddx doc audit` is not clean in this worktree. It reports repo-wide pre-existing issues:
  - many `duplicate_id` findings from mirrored docs under `docs/` and `.agents/skills/docs/`
  - one `missing_dep` for `helix.workflow` in the `.agents` copy of the principles doc

Because the acceptance explicitly requires a clean audit, I stopped short of committing this partial spec update.

If you want, the next step is to clean up the repo-wide doc graph duplication so FEAT-008 can pass the audit gate.
